conjecture_title: Antisymmetric variant of Jaeger's weak 3-flow conjecture
paper_title: Additive bases and flows in graphs
summary: The conjecture that there exists a constant $k$ such that every directed $k$-edge-connected graph has a $\mathbb{Z}_5$-antisymmetric flow was stated in arXiv:1701.03366 (2018) as the natural antisymmetric analogue of Jaeger's weak 3-flow conjecture (resolved by Thomassen and later sharpened to 6-edge-connectivity by Lovász et al.). No follow-up paper resolving or making substantial progress on this specific conjecture was found in the indexed literature through 2026.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is 'Sparse graphs with bounded induced cycle packing number have logarithmic treewidth' by Bonamy et al. — about treewidth of Ok-free sparse graphs, entirely unrelated to Z5-antisymmetric flows. The fuzz=66 match is a false positive; the 'conjecture' referenced in the contribution note concerns a different open problem in structural graph theory.

search_queries: Z5 antisymmetric flow directed edge-connected graph conjecture Esperet Thomassé 1701.03366
antisymmetric flow Z5 Jaeger weak 3-flow conjecture graph theory solved
antisymmetric flow directed graph Z5 edge-connectivity constant 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 arXiv
notes: No follow-up found after broad search. The ScienceDirect article 'Antisymmetric flows and edge-connectivity' (DOI 10.1016/S0012-365X(03)003017) appeared in search results but returned HTTP 403 and could not be verified; its date (~2003) suggests it predates the conjecture. The Z5 antisymmetric flow problem remains open as of the review date.
